companyDescription:
Every company has a mission. What's ours? To empower every person and every organization to achieve more. We believe technology can and should be a force for good and that meaningful innovation contributes to a brighter world in the future and today. Our culture doesn’t just encourage curiosity; it embraces it. Each day we make progress together by showing up as our authentic selves. We show up with a learn-it-all mentality. We show up cheering on others, knowing their success doesn't diminish our own. We show up every day open to learning our own biases, changing our behavior, and inviting in differences. Because impact matters. 

Microsoft operates in 190 countries and is made up of approximately 228,000 passionate employees worldwide.
descriptionHtml:
As a Senior Critical Environment Technician (CET) in Microsoft’s Cloud Operations & Innovation (CO+I) team, you will maintain the critical infrastructure that keeps our Datacenters up and running. This could be anything from coordinating with supplier/vendors, working closely with Management to address operational, risk and safety situations, mentoring other CE Technicians, having a hands-on understanding on how critical environment equipment works, performing various types of maintenance, responding to onsite incidents while coordinating with other critical facilities professionals, and using telemetry and other platforms to monitor equipment performance and operations.

Microsoft’s Cloud Operations & Innovation (CO+I) is the engine that powers our cloud services. As a CO+I Critical Environment Technician, you will perform a key role in delivering the core infrastructure and foundational technologies for Microsoft's online services including Bing, Office 365, Xbox, OneDrive, and the Microsoft Azure platform. As a group, CO+I is focused on the personal and professional development of all employees and offers training and opportunities including Career Rotation Programs, Diversity & Inclusion training and events, and professional certifications.

Our infrastructure is comprised of a large global portfolio of more than 200 datacenters in 32 countries and millions of servers. Our foundation is built upon and managed by a team of subject matter experts working to support services for more than 1 billion customers and 20 million businesses in over 90 countries worldwide.

With environmental sustainability and optimization at the forefront of our datacenter design and operations, we continue to grow and evolve as we meet the ever-changing business demands that hold Microsoft as a world-class cloud provider.

Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond.

Responsibilities

Responsibilities:

  • Serve as an operations specialist in one major area of CE operations (e.g., electrical, mechanical, controls, generators) and works on advanced tasks independently.
  • Inspect and supervise critical environment-related facility equipment (e.g., controls, heating, ventilation, and air conditioning [HVAC], mechanical systems), building, and grounds regularly for unsafe or abnormal conditions and escalate per applicable policies and standards.
  • Utilize internal computerized maintenance management system (CMMS) to track all equipment assets and to complete work order requests for maintenance work.
  • Safely and quickly respond to and leads onsite incident response teams for all abnormal conditions that impact operations, and coordinates with other critical facilities professionals to perform corrective repairs without supervision.
  • Enhance, develop new or follow preexisting emergency operating procedures (EOPs), methods of procedure (MOPs), and standard operating procedures (SOPs) in relation to CE operations and incidents.
  • Perform various types of maintenance (e.g., planned, predictive, corrective) and repairs following methods of procedure (MOPs), and standard operating procedures (SOPs) for CE equipment (e.g., electrical, mechanical, cooling systems) and escalate when appropriate.
  • Have a hands-on understanding of how equipment works and how to troubleshoot equipment, systems, subsystems, and components independently within their trained discipline(s).
  • Provides consultation to lower-level colleagues in troubleshooting systems and problems.
  • Understands, follows, and ensures safety and security requirements (e.g., job hazard assessments [JHAs], environmental health and safety (EH&S), toolbox talks), and business processes and procedures to properly perform work in a safe, quality, and reliable manner in accordance with applicable federal, state, local, and Microsoft requirements.
  • Embody our culture and values

Qualifications

Required Qualifications:

  • High School Diploma, GED, or equivalent AND extensive experience with mission critical services work/applied learning (e.g., high availability assembly/manufacturing/critical infrastructure environments such as data centers, oil and gas refineries, hospitals, pharmaceutical, manufacturing) OR equivalent experience.
  • Relevant experience in a specialized area (e.g., mechanical field, electrical field, controls field) or related field.

Background Check Requirements

Ability to meet Microsoft, customer and/or government security screening requirements are required for this role. These requirements include, but are not limited to the following specialized security screenings:

  • Microsoft Cloud Background Check: This position will be required to pass the Microsoft Cloud background check upon hire/transfer and every two years thereafter.

While not required, we also look for the following Preferred Qualifications:

  • High School Diploma, GED, or equivalent AND extensive experience in mission critical services (e.g., high-availability assembly/manufacturing/critical infrastructure environments such as data centers, oil and gas refineries, hospitals, pharmaceutical, manufacturing, or related fields)
  • OR Associate's Degree or technical trade certification (e.g., military, trade school), or higher-equivalent education AND significant proficiency in mission-critical services (e.g., high-availability assembly/manufacturing/critical infrastructure environments such as data centers, oil and gas refineries, hospitals, pharmaceutical, manufacturing, or related fields) OR equivalent experience.

Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. Consistent with applicable law, all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application process, read more about requesting accommodations.

companyDescription:
We deliver. PostNord is the leading supplier of communications and logistics solutions to, from and within the Nordic region. We also ensure the postal service to households and businesses in Sweden and Denmark. 

With our expertise and a strong distribution network, we develop options for tomorrow’s communication, e-commerce, distribution and logistics in the Nordic region. In 2014, PostNord had 38,000 employees and sales of SEK 40 billion. The parent company is a Swedish public limited company headquartered in Solna, Sweden.
companySlogan: Vi gör vardagen enklare.
descriptionHtml:
Do you want to shape the future of IT observability in the leading communications and logistics company in the Nordics? Are you passionate about building reliable platforms that help product teams gain insight into their systems? Then this could be the role for you!

You, Us & The Job

As an observability specialist, you will be at the forefront of our transformation, bringing new perspectives and ideas that help us rethink how we work with technology. Your expertise will be central in tackling challenges and shaping the role observability plays in supporting PostNord’s complex IT landscape.

Within Group IT, the central Observability & Monitoring team helps deliver the insights that make this possible — turning metrics, logs, traces, and dashboards into tools that enable more than 70 product teams to build reliable and transparent services. We foster a culture of innovation, knowledge sharing, and continuous improvement. As an Observability Engineer, you will help us evolve and operate our observability platform while driving PostNord’s observability maturity to the next level.

What will you do?

In this role, you will be responsible for ensuring that our observability platform is stable, scalable, and cost-efficient, while supporting teams across PostNord to succeed with observability best practices.

Your Tasks Will Include

  • Operate and evolve the observability platform – keep our stack (Grafana Cloud, Loki, Thanos/Mimir, Tempo, Pyroscope) reliable, scalable, and cost-efficient while continuously improving features and integrations.
  • Enable product teams with better insights – help over 70 teams instrument their applications with high-quality metrics, logs, traces, and SLOs, ensuring observability becomes a natural part of development and operations.
  • Build tailored observability solutions – for teams lacking the skills or resources to do it themselves.
  • Secure platform performance across hybrid infrastructure – optimize and troubleshoot workloads running in Kubernetes and on-prem, ensuring smooth operations in a complex hybrid landscape.
  • Automate for resilience – design and implement monitoring, alerting, and incident response workflows that reduce toil, speed up detection, and improve recovery times.
  • Drive collaboration through data – work closely with developers, infrastructure, and platform teams to diagnose issues, improve system reliability, and make decisions based on observability data.
  • Contribute to maturity and standards – document best practices, create guidelines, and help raise observability capabilities across the organization.

We are looking for a collaborative, proactive, and service-minded engineer who enjoys enabling others through reliable platforms. You are curious and adaptable, with a strong technical foundation and a structured way of working. You take responsibility for your tasks, communicate clearly, and contribute positively to team culture.

Your Experience

  • Strong knowledge of Linux system administration.
  • Extensive experience with observability tools such as Grafana, Prometheus/Mimir, Loki, Tempo, or similar.
  • Experience with Kubernetes (OpenShift/EKS) and containerized workloads.
  • Familiarity with cloud platforms (Azure/AWS) and hybrid IT environments.
  • Scripting/automation skills (e.g., Python, Go, Bash).
  • Fluent in English (Swedish is a plus).

You should furthermore have experience with infrastructure-as-code tools such as Terraform, Helm, or ArgoCD, as well as familiarity with profiling solutions like Pyroscope. Certifications in cloud platforms, Kubernetes, or observability technologies are considered meritorious.

We offer you

At PostNord, we are a modern, customer-oriented company that cares as much about our employees as we do about our customers. With us, you’ll take on a varied role in an international environment, facing exciting challenges and benefiting from quick decision-making.

In addition to fair employment conditions under collective agreements, we offer:

  • Flexibility at work – a modern hybrid workplace (watch the film about our office)
  • Room to grow – excellent opportunities for personal and professional development
  • Security for the future – strong insurance and occupational pension benefits
  • Health & wellness – wellness allowance and free access to Arken’s (PostNord HQ) gym and swimming pool
  • Exclusive perks – employee benefits through PostNord Plus, our own personnel foundation

About Us

PostNord is the leading supplier of communication and logistics solutions to, from, and within the Nordic region. We ensure the provision of a postal service to households and businesses in Sweden and Denmark. We aim to make everyday life easier and more sustainable for everyone who lives and works in the Nordic region. We are in a constant transformation and work consistently to develop both our offer and our employees, all to achieve our vision of being the favourite carrier of the Nordics. We are PostNord - a fantastic company to work and grow with. Read more at group.postnord.com



Apply

Welcome to submit your application. The selection is ongoing, so please apply as soon as possible. If you have any questions or concerns, please email marcus.nilsson@postnord.com or call +46702249216.

We look forward to hearing from you!

Read more about our union representatives

companyDescription:
SAVR is a Swedish investment platform founded in 2019, with the aim of transforming the financial industry through innovative solutions and a sharp focus on user needs. As the first investment service in Sweden to offer commission-free funds, SAVR set a new standard in the market. In the fall of 2024, we introduced stock trading with automated commissions, marking another significant step in our ongoing journey.

SAVR’s objective is to empower individuals to grow their investments by challenging traditional models with innovative solutions, fair pricing, and valuable insights. With over 100,000 customers, SAVR continues to disrupt the industry, driving innovation to create a fairer and more accessible investment market.
companySlogan: SAVR is an investment platform looking to revolutionize the world of savings.
descriptionHtml:
SAVR is a fast-growing Swedish fintech company on a mission to reinvent investing. We empower investors with a platform that offers lower fees, greater transparency, and a radically improved experience.


We’re not just building another investment service — we’re reimagining how financial products should work for people, not just institutions. As part of our ambitious, innovative and curious team, you’ll help shape the future of investing in Sweden and beyond.


The Role

As a Senior Infrastructure Engineer with security skills in our Backend team, you will be part of our tech team based in Stockholm or Halmstad. You will be instrumental in building and developing the next generation investment platform, with a strong focus on cloud infrastructure and security operations (DevSecOps).


You will report directly to our VP of Engineering and your work will be supported by our Tech Team Lead. Our robust tech stack, crucial for our cloud infrastructure, consists of Terraform, Kotlin, Python, SQL, and PHP(Laravel), and is hosted on AWS. We emphasize DevSecOps principles throughout our development lifecycle.


Your Mission

  • Advance and optimize our cloud infrastructure, ensuring scalability, efficiency, and reliability.
  • Enhance and implement our security practices, integrating DevSecOps principles to secure feature development.
  • Build and deploy robust software features within a fast-paced, agile environment.
  • Drive and develop innovative technical concepts and solutions from inception to implementation.
  • Actively contribute to the continuous improvement of our tech stack, architectural designs, and operational methodologies.
  • Collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams to build high-quality products, adhering to best engineering practices.
  • Develop and maintain secure integrations with third-party APIs.
  • Foster our learning culture by actively participating in feedback sessions, both receiving and providing constructive feedback on code.


You’ll Thrive in This Role If You...

  • Are a motivated engineer with a passion for cloud infrastructure and platform engineering, and a security-first mindset within the backend space.
  • Have 4+ years of team-based experience building and operating scalable, product-grade systems.
  • Have strong in AWS and Infrastructure as Code (Terraform), with experience in CI/CD, containerization (ECS/EKS/Docker), networking, and observability (metrics, logging, tracing).
  • Are skilled at improving performance, reliability, and cost efficiency through automation, autoscaling, and incident response practices.
  • Integrate security into infrastructure by applying least-privilege IAM, secrets management, vulnerability scanning, secure CI/CD pipelines, and well-monitored API integrations.
  • Are able to collaborate closely with backend teams and contribute directly to codebases (Kotlin, Python, PHP/Laravel, SQL) when needed.
  • Are a team player who values feedback, communicates fluently in English, and balances speed with long-term maintainability.
  • Eligibility: Swedish citizen or valid EU work permit (visa sponsorship not available).


If your experience doesn't align perfectly with every bullet point, but you possess strong skills in cloud infrastructure, platform engineering, or security, and you have a genuine growth mindset, we still encourage you to apply. Tell us how your unique skills and passion will help us build a secure, scalable investment platform at SAVR. We'd love to hear from you.


Our Cultural DNA

At SAVR, we’re:

🧠 Open-minded – we embrace new ideas, diverse perspectives, and continuous learning

🚀 Bold – we take smart risks, move fast, and aren’t afraid to fail forward

🤝 Honest – we value transparency, feedback, and doing the right thing


We’re a startup, so things can be a little messy — and that’s part of the thrill. If you thrive in building as a team and handling uncertainty, you will fit right in.


What’s In It for You

You’ll join a product- and design-driven company where your work has real impact. We keep things lean, move fast, and genuinely care about the experience we deliver — for customers and for each other.


Here’s what we offer:

💻 Top-tier work gear

💰 Competitive salary

🏃 ♀️ Generous wellness allowance

🤒 Insurance package

🌱 Occupational pension and long-term savings support

🏢 Office-first approach to foster collaboration and quick decision-making — with flexibility when you need it (4+1)

🥗 Stocked pantry, plus regular social gatherings and wellness activities to keep energy and culture high


Our Commitment to Diversity

SAVR is an equal opportunity employer. We welcome people from all backgrounds, identities, and experiences. We believe diverse teams build better products and are committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive.


Ready to Join Us?

We’d love to hear from you. Apply now or reach out with any questions — and let’s build something meaningful together.

title: Senior Infrastructure and Security Engineer

companyDescription: Prevent cyber breach and minimize impact
companySlogan: Prevent cyber breach and minimize impact
descriptionHtml:
Do you want to be at the forefront of protecting our society against cyber threats? We are looking for a Senior Security Engineer/Architect to team up with the most dedicated team of cyber specialists in our Managed Security Services.

About Truesec – A Leading Cybersecurity Company

Since 2005, Truesec has been a native cybersecurity company, driven by one single purpose: Creating safety and sustainability in a digital world by preventing cyber breach and minimizing impact.

Over the years, we’ve earned the trust of organizations worldwide and gained a strong reputation internationally. Our team consists of dedicated specialists covering the entire spectrum of cybersecurity – with capabilities in Predict, Prevent, Protect, Respond, and Recover.

Today, we employ around 400 employees – and as cybercrime grows, we need to grow. To support that goal, we are now looking for a Senior Security/Architect who wants to make a difference in our Managed Security Services business unit.

The Role

In this position, you will play a pivotal role in designing and implementing advanced security architectures for some of the Nordics’ most critical organizations. You will take end-to-end responsibility for hardening and safeguarding complex environments, combining strategic vision with hands-on execution.

Your deep understanding of building secure infrastructures (both in theory and practice) will be instrumental in identifying, preventing, and managing sophisticated threats. As a bridge between our detection engineers and solution architects, you will also serve as a vital link to our CTO and Technology organization.

Here, you will have the opportunity to work with cutting-edge technologies and directly influence the security strategy of both Truesec and our clients.

Your Potential Background

You bring over 10 years of experience in cybersecurity, with a proven track record of preventing breaches and minimizing their impact across entire IT environments. You have also developed security architectures for complex organizations. Practical experience from defense authorities or critical infrastructure is highly advantageous. You possess both theoretical and practical expertise in building and securing environments against today’s and tomorrow’s threats. You excel as a technical leader and mentor, and are comfortable collaborating across disciplines to drive security initiatives forward.

Required Skills:

  • Deep technical knowledge, extensive hands-on and strategic experience preventing breaches, including architecture, implementation, and operations.
  • Ability to design and continuously improve security strategies and processes for detection, response, and prevention.
  • Solid experience with security tools and platforms (SIEM, EDR, NDR, etc.) and advanced proficiency in at least one query language (e.g., KQL, SQL).
  • Strong awareness of the threat landscape, attack techniques (MITRE ATT&CK), vulnerabilities, and countermeasures in both IT and OT environments.
  • Ability to work closely with other experts and communicate complex solutions clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
  • Demonstrated experience leading and executing high-quality, confidential security projects.

Bonus Skills:

  • Experience working with or within defense organizations or critical infrastructure sectors.
  • Experience of Microsoft Security Suite
  • Practical threat hunting and incident response expertise.
  • Proficiency in scripting and automation (e.g., Python, PowerShell).
  • In-depth knowledge of Linux and macOS internals, including system processes, logging, and attack vectors.
  • Experience with cloud security and detection in environments such as AWS, Azure, or GCP.
  • Familiarity with integrating threat intelligence feeds into detection strategies.
  • Exposure to machine learning and behavioral analytics for advanced detection.
  • Experience from delivering managed services is a plus.

Due to the sensitivity in our assignments, we require Swedish citizenship, citizenship in an EU country, or a Swedish work permit.

Join Our Mission Toward a Safe Digital Future

If you truly want to make a difference and make our world a little bit safer, this is the place for you. At Truesec, you'll get to be at the forefront of fighting cybercrime instead of reading about it in the news. This is your chance to be part of an exciting journey in a leading cybersecurity company - and learn from some of the best in their field.

So, are you ready to team up with the most dedicated team of experts and be part of a meaningful mission? Then we are excited to get to know you!

title: Senior Security Engineer/Architect

companyDescription: RELEX Solutions delivers a unified platform for retail, manufacturing, and supply chain planning, enabled by proven AI technology. We help retailers, manufacturers, and consumer goods companies optimize demand forecasting, replenishment, merchandising, pricing and promotions, supply chain operations, and production planning across the end-to-end value chain. Companies like ADUSA, AutoZone, Coles, Circle K, Dollar Tree and Family Dollar, M&S Food, PetSmart, Rituals, The Home Depot, and Systemair trust RELEX to increase product availability, boost sales, deliver actionable insights, improve sustainability, and drive profitable growth. Learn more at: https://www.relexsolutions.com/customers/
companySlogan: Delivering a unified supply chain planning platform for retailers and manufacturers, enabled by proven AI technology
descriptionHtml:
RELEX Solutions create cutting-edge optimisation software to help retailers and consumer brands drive profitable growth. With growth comes opportunities, and we embrace both. Within our platforms, our teams are driving change, working with international colleagues and the latest tech stack to develop solutions that transform into a pioneering end product; it’s tangible and impactful – for our customers and the world.

Our Technology Team

Our Technology team enjoys a challenge. They’re hungry to learn and don’t hesitate to ask what, why, and how. They get to work with various technologies to create high-quality, scalable applications (just imagine, thousands of stores, millions of products, and billions of rows of raw data!). Their expertise positively impacts the environment and business processes around the world; alongside international colleagues, they drive change and develop solutions that become our pioneering end product. From functional programming, machine learning, and interesting algorithms, to building big data pipelines, they’re behind it all. They can work flexibly, remote or in-office – after all, they contribute to all our achievements, so we reward them with genuine work-life balance and access to every opportunity.

Your Role:

We are now looking for a Security Architect, Plan Engineering to join our company of extraordinary people. As a Software Security Architect, your mission will be to safeguard our customers' highly confidential business data and ensure our services remain continuously available. You will have considerable freedom in prioritizing your own work, while being backed by our seasoned information security team that provides you with guardrails,

At RELEX we empower engineering teams to own their security controls and continuously improve them, although we recognize that sometimes they may need guidance or support. As Security Architect, you'll be instrumental in bridging this gap, helping teams enhance their security posture while maintaining our high standards of data protection and system resilience.

To succeed in your role, you need to have good communications skills to sell your ideas inside the product development organization.

What you will be doing:

As a Security Architect at RELEX Solutions, you will ensure our systems are secure and resilient. You will design functional security controls; ensure non-functional security requirements are met and act as a trusted advisor to engineering teams.

  • Design Functional Security Architecture: Develop and implement security controls that protect our systems, participate in roadmap prioritization
  • Support Security Audits: Support the teams in preparing for independent security audits
  • Incident Response Readiness: Design systems that are prepared to detect, respond to, and enable effective investigation of security incidents
  • Security Testing Processes: Establish and orchestrate common processes for SAST, DAST, and SCA
  • Threat Modeling: Collaborate with engineering teams to discover threats and suggest remediations
  • Trusted Advisor: Act as a point of contact and advisor for security-related matters within the engineering teams

What you bring to the table:

  • Strong expertise in Azure, hybrid cloud, Kubernetes, and the Java ecosystem
  • Experience in building secure applications on Linux and defending Linux environments
  • Proven ability to design and drive the implementation of robust security measures
  • Capability to read and reason about code
  • Aptitude to automate yourself out of repetitive situations using scripting
  • Excellent communication skills, with the ability to effectively collaborate and influence within a product development environment
  • Fluency in English, with strong literacy and writing skills

What we consider an advantage:

  • Experience with CSPM (Wiz), Hashicorp Vault, JFrog, or SonarQube.
  • Familiarity with the latest security trends and threats.
  • Strong problem-solving skills with attention to detail.
  • Experience with security standards such as ISO 27001 and ISAE 3000 SOC 2.

As part of a truly international team, you’ll collaborate across time zones and cultures, driving innovation and achieving shared success. Our Product & Technology hubs are in Finland, Sweden, Lisbon, the UK, and the US, fostering global collaboration. Our modern approach ensures that work is meaningful, inclusive, and impactful, no matter where you are.

Our employees deserve fair and competitive pay that meets them where they are. With scalable benefits, rewards, and perks, our total rewards programs reflect our commitment to inclusivity and access for all. We actively listen to our community to continuously improve our offerings, ensuring they remain relevant and valuable as we design and update them.

Key Benefits

  • We work in a collaborative environment, deploy continuously, and tackle real-world challenges that impact thousands of users every day.
  • The chance to influence technology choices and grow the mobile app ecosystem at RELEX.
  • Flexible and hybrid working options to support work-life integration.
  • Comprehensive health and wellness initiatives.
  • Professional development opportunities, including training, mentorship, and clear career pathways.
  • Generous leave policies, including parental leave and paid time off, supporting both personal and professional priorities.

Life at RELEX

Life at RELEX is built on collaboration, respect, and flexibility. We believe in fostering a work environment where innovation thrives and everyone feels valued. Our teams work together to solve meaningful challenges, creating an inclusive culture that supports both professional and personal growth. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for everyone - regardless of background, identity, or experience. Whether you’re engaging with colleagues across the globe or finding your own work-life balance, life at RELEX is about making an impact while enjoying the journey. What difference will you make?

Apply today and become a part of RELEX!

Note that we will fill this position as soon as we’ve found the right person, so we recommend that you act quickly.

With scalable benefits, rewards, and perks, our total rewards programs reflect our commitment to inclusivity and access for all. We actively listen to our community to continuously improve our offerings, ensuring they remain relevant and valuable as we design and update them.Key BenefitsWe work in a collaborative environment, deploy continuously, and tackle real-world challenges that impact thousands of users every day.The chance to influence technology choices and grow the mobile app ecosystem at RELEX.Flexible and hybrid working options to support work-life integration.Comprehensive health and wellness initiatives.Professional development opportunities, including training, mentorship, and clear career pathways.Generous leave policies, including parental leave and paid time off, supporting both personal and professional priorities.Life at RELEXLife at RELEX is built on collaboration, respect, and flexibility. We believe in fostering a work environment where innovation thrives and everyone feels valued. Our teams work together to solve meaningful challenges, creating an inclusive culture that supports both professional and personal growth. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for everyone - regardless of background, identity, or experience. Whether you’re engaging with colleagues across the globe or finding your own work-life balance, life at RELEX is about making an impact while enjoying the journey. What difference will you make?Apply today and become a part of RELEX!Note that we will fill this position as soon as we’ve found the right person, so we recommend that you act quickly.

companyDescription:
We're championing possibilities for all by making money fast, easy, and more enjoyable. Our hope is unlock opportunities for people in their everyday lives and empower the millions of people and businesses around the world who trust, rely, and use PayPal every day. 

For support, visit the PayPal Help Center. https://payp.al/help

For employment opportunities, check out our job openings in the 'Jobs' tab. We're an equal opportunity employer that welcomes diversity, and offer generous benefits to help you thrive at work and in your free time.
descriptionHtml:
The Company
PayPal has been revolutionizing commerce globally for more than 25 years. Creating innovative experiences that make moving money, selling, and shopping simple, personalized, and secure, PayPal empowers consumers and businesses in approximately 200 markets to join and thrive in the global economy.

We operate a global, two-sided network at scale that connects hundreds of millions of merchants and consumers. We help merchants and consumers connect, transact, and complete payments, whether they are online or in person. PayPal is more than a connection to third-party payment networks. We provide proprietary payment solutions accepted by merchants that enable the completion of payments on our platform on behalf of our customers.

We offer our customers the flexibility to use their accounts to purchase and receive payments for goods and services, as well as the ability to transfer and withdraw funds. We enable consumers to exchange funds more safely with merchants using a variety of funding sources, which may include a bank account, a PayPal or Venmo account balance, PayPal and Venmo branded credit products, a credit card, a debit card, certain cryptocurrencies, or other stored value products such as gift cards, and eligible credit card rewards. Our PayPal, Venmo, and Xoom products also make it safer and simpler for friends and family to transfer funds to each other. We offer merchants an end-to-end payments solution that provides authorization and settlement capabilities, as well as instant access to funds and payouts. We also help merchants connect with their customers, process exchanges and returns, and manage risk. We enable consumers to engage in cross-border shopping and merchants to extend their global reach while reducing the complexity and friction involved in enabling cross-border trade.

Our beliefs are the foundation for how we conduct business every day. We live each day guided by our core values of Inclusion, Innovation, Collaboration, and Wellness. Together, our values ensure that we work together as one global team with our customers at the center of everything we do – and they push us to ensure we take care of ourselves, each other, and our communities.

Job Description Summary:
Are you a technically savvy leader ready to drive real impact? We're hiring an Engineering Manager to lead our new Commerce Platform team and shape the future of merchant experiences. The team builds practical, high-impact solutions that help merchants streamline inventory and order management—whether they’re selling online, in-store, or both. We work across disciplines, blending strong engineering fundamentals with modern tools like Java, Kotlin, and React to deliver innovative, user-friendly experiences that make commerce simpler and smarter.

Job Description:
Our product empowers businesses to tackle complex challenges with surprising ease, think enterprise-grade power wrapped in delightfully intuitive tech.

As part of this team, you won’t just be building features, you’ll be laying the foundation for commerce capabilities across PayPal. And with the rise of agentic commerce (yes, the buzz is real and it’s so hot right now), your work will be right at the heart of what’s next in the future of digital transactions.

What You Need To Know About The Role
  • Lead and mentor a team of software engineers working on payment systems, ensuring high performance, engagement, and growth.
  • Collaborate closely with Product, Compliance, and Finance teams to define and deliver payment features and improvements.
  • Oversee the design, development, and deployment of scalable and secure payment solutions.
  • Drive technical excellence through code reviews, architecture discussions, and continuous improvement initiatives.
  • Manage sprint planning, stand-ups, retrospectives, and other agile ceremonies to ensure smooth delivery.
  • Monitor system performance and reliability, and lead incident response and root cause analysis when needed.
  • Partner with stakeholders to define technical roadmaps and align engineering efforts with business goals.
  • Foster a culture of innovation, accountability, and continuous learning within the team.
  • Stay up to date with trends in the payments industry and evaluate new technologies or vendors as needed.


What You Need To Bring
  • Proven experience in team leadership and mentoring.
  • Coding experience in Java, Kotlin, React, or similar technologies.
  • Familiarity with Agile development methodologies.
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Proven experience leading cross-functional engineering teams in a fast-paced fintech or payments environment.
  • Ability to mentor and grow engineers through coaching, feedback, and career development planning.
  • Skilled in agile methodologies (Scrum, Kanban) and driving continuous delivery and improvement.
  • Experience in setting technical direction and aligning engineering goals with business objectives.
  • Track record of building scalable, resilient, and high-availability systems.
  • Experience with observability tools (e.g., Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog) and incident management.
  • Strong focus on quality, testing, and automation in CI/CD pipelines.


PayPal does not charge candidates any fees for courses, applications, resume reviews, interviews, background checks, or onboarding. Any such request is a red flag and likely part of a scam. To learn more about how to identify and avoid recruitment fraud please visit https://careers.pypl.com/contact-us.

For the majority of employees, PayPal's balanced hybrid work model offers 3 days in the office for effective in-person collaboration and 2 days at your choice of either the PayPal office or your home workspace, ensuring that you equally have the benefits and conveniences of both locations.

Our Benefits:

At PayPal, we’re committed to building an equitable and inclusive global economy. And we can’t do this without our most important asset-you. That’s why we offer benefits to help you thrive in every stage of life. We champion your financial, physical, and mental health by offering valuable benefits and resources to help you care for the whole you.

We have great benefits including a flexible work environment, employee shares options, health and life insurance and more. To learn more about our benefits please visit https://www.paypalbenefits.com

Who We Are:

To learn more about our culture and community visit https://about.pypl.com/who-we-are/default.aspx

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

PayPal provides equal employment opportunity (EEO) to all persons regardless of age, color, national origin, citizenship status, physical or mental disability, race, religion, creed, gender, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, genetic information, marital status, status with regard to public assistance, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. In addition, PayPal will provide reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities. If you are unable to submit an application because of incompatible assistive technology or a disability, please contact us at paypalglobaltalentacquisition@paypal.com.

Belonging at PayPal:

Our employees are central to advancing our mission, and we strive to create an environment where everyone can do their best work with a sense of purpose and belonging. Belonging at PayPal means creating a workplace with a sense of acceptance and security where all employees feel included and valued. We are proud to have a diverse workforce reflective of the merchants, consumers, and communities that we serve, and we continue to take tangible actions to cultivate inclusivity and belonging at PayPal.

Any general requests for consideration of your skills, please Join our Talent Community.

We know the confidence gap and imposter syndrome can get in the way of meeting spectacular candidates. Please don’t hesitate to apply.

companyDescription:
KPMG is a global network of professional firms providing audit, tax and advisory services. We operate in 143 countries and have nearly 265 000 
 people working in member firms around the world. KPMG Sweden has 1650 employees in offices around Sweden. With a worldwide presence, KPMG continues to build on its success thanks to clear vision, defined values and, above all, its people.
companySlogan: KPMG är ett av världens ledande kunskapsföretag. Vi jobbar för att göra skillnad & förbättra framtiden för våra kunder.
descriptionHtml:
Har du ett skarpt säkerhetstänk och en passion för teknik? Vill du vara med och hjälpa organisationer att snabbare upptäcka, hantera och åtgärda säkerhetshot – med hjälp av ServiceNow? Är du nyfiken på hur automatisering och smarta integrationer kan lyfta säkerhetsarbetet till nästa nivå? Då är det dig vi söker till vår växande ServiceNow-practice inom Security Operations!

Om oss

På KPMG utökar vi vår ServiceNow-practice, med fokus på att leverera digitala lösningar inom bl a risk, IT, HR och regelefterlevnad. Divisionen Advisory består av konsulter med bred expertis – från teknik och juridik till ekonomi och hållbarhet. Tillsammans hjälper vi organisationer i både offentlig och privat sektor att driva transformation och skapa verkligt affärsvärde.

Vår mångfald i både människor och kompetenser gör oss till en ledande rådgivare på marknaden. Vi arbetar med högpresterande kunder inom alla sektorer, inklusive privat sektor, offentlig sektor och finansiella tjänster. En viktig del av vårt arbete är digitalisering och integrering av ny teknik för att förändra riskhanterings- och efterlevnadsprocesser för våra kunder. Vi ger råd till våra kunder i deras transformationsresor och främjar digitala, innovativa och hållbara lösningar på våra kunders affärsutmaningar.

Vi hjälper våra kunder med rådgivning och implementering av den ledande teknikplattformen ServiceNow, för digitalisering och transformation inom våra discipliner. Våra verksamhetsområden inkluderar riskhantering, säkerhet, supply chain, HR, portföljhantering, leverantörsstyrning och utveckling av kundspecifika lösningar.

KPMG:s ServiceNow-satsning är en global framgångssaga och vårt fokus på professionell expertis och domänexpertis har gjort oss till en föredragen partner, både för ServiceNow och för våra kunder. Våra insatser har uppmärksammats globalt genom att bland annat ha utsetts till ServiceNow Global Transformation Partner of the Year 2025.

Din roll

Som ServiceNow SecOps-konsult kommer du att spela en central roll i att hjälpa våra kunder att effektivisera och stärka sitt säkerhetsarbete med stöd av ServiceNow-plattformen. Du kommer att arbeta med lösningar som integrerar säkerhetsdata, automatiserar processer och förbättrar incidentrespons.

Tjänsten kan vara placerad vid något av våra kontor i Stockholm, Göteborg eller Malmö.

Dina uppgifter kommer bland annat att omfatta:

  • Rådgivning och implementering av ServiceNow Security Operations (SecOps) moduler, såsom Security Incident Response (SIR), Vulnerability Response (VR) och Threat Intelligence
  • Analysera och kartlägga kunders säkerhetsprocesser och översätta dessa till effektiva tekniska lösningar
  • Konfigurera, anpassa och integrera ServiceNow för att möta kundspecifika behov
  • Arbeta nära kundens säkerhets-, IT- och riskteam för att möjliggöra helhetliga och effektiva säkerhetsflöden
  • Bidra i hela projektcykeln – från förstudie och design till test, driftsättning och förvaltning
  • Vara en del av vår växande ServiceNow-practice och bidra till kunskapsdelning, metodutveckling och communitybyggande i Norden och EMA

Det finns stort utrymme att växa professionellt och personligt i denna position!

,

Vi tror att du har:

  • Minst 3–5 års erfarenhet inom cybersäkerhet, IT-säkerhet eller säkerhetsrelaterade processer
  • God förståelse för ServiceNow eller andra ITSM/ITOM-plattformar
  • Erfarenhet av arbete med säkerhetsincidenter, sårbarhetshantering eller hotanalys
  • Förmåga att förstå och förklara komplexa tekniska samband på ett affärsmässigt sätt
  • Relevant akademisk utbildning inom IT, teknik eller cybersäkerhet
  • God kommunikationsförmåga, både på svenska och engelska

Meriterande:

  • Erfarenhet av implementation eller konfiguration av ServiceNow SecOps-moduler (t.ex. SIR, VR)
  • Certifiering i ServiceNow (t.ex. Certified Implementation Specialist – Security Incident Response)
  • Erfarenhet från konsultrollen och arbete i kundprojekt
  • Kunskap om ramverk som NIST, MITRE ATT&CK, ISO 27001 eller CIS Controls
  • Förståelse för integration av säkerhetsverktyg (t.ex. SIEM, CMDB, threat feeds) med ServiceNow

För att trivas och lyckas hos oss behöver du vara en lagspelare med en stark vilja att driva digital innovation. Du är redo att bidra till vårt gemensamma mål att vara bäst i branschen. Din förmåga att lyssna och bygga förtroende, i kombination med ett proaktivt förhållningssätt till utmaningar, kommer att göra dig till en värdefull del av vårt team. Vi söker dig som trivs med ett högt tempo, hanterar flera processer samtidigt och som alltid letar efter nya lösningar.

Vill du göra skillnad med oss?

Välkommen med din ansökan, vi behöver ditt CV samt ett personligt brev. Observera att rekryteringsprocessen kommer att vara tillfälligt pausad under sommarens semesterveckor och fortsätta i mitten av augusti.

Om du har några frågor om tjänsten, tveka inte att kontakta rekryterande chef Emil Lundvall, emil.lundvall@kpmg.se. För eventuella frågor relaterade till rekryteringsprocessen, vänligen kontakta Talent Acquisition Partner, Marie-Louise Bååth, marie-louise.baath@kpmg.se

Vi ser fram emot din ansökan och för nya kollegor att haka på vår resa!

I den här rekryteringen har vi följande steg;

  • Verksamhetsintervju
  • Personlighet- och logisk test med personlighetsintervju
  • Slutintervju
  • Referenser
  • Bakgrundskontroll

companyDescription:
Vi på Green Cargo erbjuder näringslivet säkra, kapacitetsstarka och hållbara transporter till cirka 200 platser i Skandinavien och med partners når vi närmare 1000 i övriga Europa. Vårt transportarbete uppgick till 9,7 miljarder nettotonkm 2024. Nästan 98 procent av vårt totala transportarbete sker med eldrivna lok där elen uteslutande kommer från fossilfria källor. Varje vardag kör vi 31 miljoner nettotonkilometer och ersätter därmed 9 000 lastbilstransporter i vägnätet. Vi har 1 750 anställda, och har en årsomsättning på 4,2 miljarder SEK (2024).

Våra kunder finns inom basindustrin där segment som skog, stål och papper dominerar, men där även kemi och energi, verkstads- och fordonsindustri samt dagligvaruhandel står för betydande volymer. Ett växande segment är intermodala godsflöden av framförallt container- och trailertransporter. Stora transportflöden via exempelvis Sveriges hamnar ger upphov till behov av landtransporter där Green Cargo har en stark position.
companySlogan: Raka spåret till en klimatsmart affär
descriptionHtml:
Som Chef för IT-Drift hos Green Cargo i Stockholm eller Hallsberg kommer du att driva transformationen i en komplex molnmiljö! Vi söker nu en nyckelperson som med helhetssyn och strategisk höjd vill leda arbetet med IT-Drift för ett företag som bokstavligen får svensk industri och handel att rulla på. Som Chef för IT-Drift kommer du spela en avgörande roll i vår digitala transformation som vi nu står inför – och tillsammans med vårt team och våra partners skapa framtidens transportlösningar. Vi erbjuder dig Hos Green Cargo blir du en del av ett företag med ett tydligt syfte – att göra skillnad för både klimatet och samhället genom hållbara logistiklösningar. Vår verksamhet är i ständig utveckling och du får vara med på en spännande förändringsresa där digitalisering och innovation står i centrum. Vi erbjuder:

  • En värderingsstyrd organisation med fokus på hållbarhet, ansvarstagande och samarbete.
  • En inkluderande och öppen arbetskultur där vi värdesätter kunskapsutbyte, transparens och gemensam utveckling.
  • En arbetsmiljö med balans mellan tydliga mål och personlig frihet, där du ges förtroende att driva ditt uppdrag.
  • Flexibla arbetsformer med möjlighet till hybridarbete och kontorsplacering i Solna eller Hallsberg.
  • En IT-organisation som har vunnit IDC Future Digital Enterprise Award, Best in Digital Infrastructure, EMEA, samt erhållit svenska och internationella ledarskapsutmärkelser. Det här gör du hos oss Som Chef IT-Drift ansvarar du för att leda transformationen av hela driftsområdet – från infrastruktur till säkerhet, licenshantering och kontinuerlig förbättring.

Du kommer bland annat att:

  • Leda transformationen av molntjänster och säkerställa en effektiv migrering och integration av komplexa systembyten.
  • Implementera strategier för att optimera framtida IT-drift och säkerställa att verksamheten är redo för framtida teknologiska utmaningar.
  • Leda och utveckla teamet inom IT-Drift, inklusive personalansvar och kompetensutveckling.
  • Rapportera direkt till CIO och ta en aktiv roll i ITs ledningsgrupp.
  • Proaktivt bidra till komplexa IT-projekt genom att analysera och optimera Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) för framtida IT-drift.
  • Säkerställa daglig drift och en robust infrastrukturmiljö – både i molnet och i samverkan med outsourcade partners.
  • Utveckla processer inom Incident, Problem och Change management enligt ITIL.
  • Driva förbättringsarbete i samverkan med verksamheten och implementera agila arbetssätt enligt SAFe.
  • Utveckla säkerhetsfunktioner inom Detect & Response, RedTeam, Blue SOC m.m.
  • Fungera som systemägare för applikationer inom IT-Drift.
  • Driva autonomi i IT-sourcing och leverantörssamverkan för att optimera leveranskedjan och säkerställa hög kvalitet och kostnadseffektivitet. Ditt team och arbetsplats Som Chef för IT Drift kommer du ha personalansvar för ett team på cirka 12 personer som består av bland annat Service Managers, Change Managers, Operation Security-specialister och Cloud Specialists. Du blir en del av ITs ledningsgrupp och arbetar nära CIO samt andra chefer inom IT-utveckling, Arkitektur och Verksamhetsstöd.

Tjänsten är placerad i Solna i Stockholm eller Hallsberg utanför Örebro, beroende på vad som passar dig bäst. Vi erbjuder en hybrid arbetsmodell med flexibilitet mellan kontorsarbete och distans. Mer om dig Vi söker dig som har gedigen erfarenhet inom IT, varav minst 5 år i ledande roller, gärna inom större organisationer och med outsourcingerfarenhet. Du har en modern ledarskapsprofil och är van och trivs som bäst när du får driva förändring i moderna och komplexa miljöer. Du är strukturerad, strategisk och lösningsorienterad. Du har lätt för att skapa relationer, är kommunikativ och trygg i ditt beslutsfattande. Vi ser också att du har:

  • Akademisk utbildning inom IT eller likvärdig erfarenhet.
  • Dokumenterad erfarenhet av molnbaserade infrastrukturer, särskilt Microsoft Azure.
  • Erfarenhet av ITIL-processer och arbete i beställarorganisationer.
  • God förståelse för IT-säkerhet och cybersäkerhet.
  • Vana att arbeta med agila metoder och ramverk såsom SAFe.
  • Flytande i svenska och engelska i tal och skrift. Meriterande:
  • Erfarenhet från transportsektorn.
  • Kunskap inom ServiceNow och IT-licenshantering.
  • Erfarenhet av att leda säkerhetstjänster såsom SOC, Red/Blue Team.
  • Kunskap i Microsoft 365, Intune och Microsoft Identity. I den här rekryteringen har vi valt att samarbeta med Ada Digital som strävar efter en transparent, inkluderande och snabbrörlig rekryteringsupplevelse.

Du söker tjänsten enkelt och behöver inte bifoga några dokument. Det räcker med en motivering till varför du söker tjänsten och om du saknar CV kan du bifoga din LinkedIn-profil. Följ sedan din ansökan live via vår hemsida.

Urvalet sker löpande och tjänsten kan bli tillsatt före sista ansökningsdatum. Som anställd hos Green Cargo genomförs slumpmässiga drogtester. Med anställningen följer en skyldighet att krigsplaceras. Är du nyfiken och vill veta mer innan du söker? Hör av dig till ansvarig rekryterare.

Vi ser fram emot att få kontakt med dig! Green Cargo erbjuder näringslivet säkra, kapacitetsstarka och hållbara transporter till cirka 200 platser i Skandinavien och med partners når vi närmare 1000 i övriga Europa. Vårt transportarbete uppgick till 9,7 miljarder nettotonkm 2024. Nästan 98 procent av vårt totala transportarbete sker med eldrivna lok där elen uteslutande kommer från fossilfria källor. Varje vardag kör vi 31 miljoner nettotonkilometer och ersätter därmed 9 000 lastbilstransporter i vägnätet. Vi har 1 750 anställda, och har en årsomsättning på 4,2 miljarder SEK (2024).

companyDescription:
Our 15 000 talented colleagues serve millions of customers every day in one of the world’s most connected regions. With a strong connectivity base, we’re the hub in the digital ecosystem, empowering people, companies and societies to stay in touch with everything that matters 24/7/365 - on their terms. 

Our goal is to make Telia better for customers, employees, owners and societies in the Nordics and the Baltics.
companySlogan: We reinvent better connected living.
descriptionHtml:
Ready to shape the future of secure connectivity?

We’re looking for a Security Specialist to strengthen our Network Functions & Security team. In this role, you’ll play a key part in safeguarding Telia’s digital infrastructure and supporting our business customers. If you’re passionate about protecting networks, driving security innovation, and making an impact in a dynamic DevSecOps environment, this could be your next step.

About The Role

As a Security Specialist, you’ll take ownership of Telia’s firewall and proxy infrastructure, ensuring our platforms remain robust, scalable, and secure. You’ll work closely with the Cybersecurity Product Unit to design and deliver security solutions, while also contributing to incident response, automation, and integration of security in new initiatives. This role combines hands-on engineering with the responsibility of keeping our networks resilient against evolving threats.

Your key responsibilities will include:

  • Managing the full lifecycle of Telia’s firewall and proxy solutions.
  • Designing and implementing secure network and cloud architectures.
  • Supporting customer-facing security services in collaboration with the Cybersecurity Product Unit.
  • Handling incident response, troubleshooting, and change management.
  • Providing third-line support, including on-call duties.
  • Developing automation scripts and improving security management tools.
  • Embedding security early in the development lifecycle of new initiatives.

What We’re Looking For...

To succeed in this role, you bring a solid mix of technical expertise and a problem-solving mindset. You enjoy working in a collaborative environment and take ownership of your work with precision and care.

Your background and skills include:

  • Degree in IT, Telecommunications, or equivalent experience.
  • Proven experience with firewalls and proxies – hands-on Check Point firewall experience is required.
  • Strong knowledge of TCP/IP networking and LAN environments.
  • Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and cloud security.
  • Familiarity with Git/GitLab, Python, or other scripting tools.
  • Several years of IT and network operations experience.

Preferred certifications:

  • Check Point Certified Security Expert (CCSE).
  • Check Point Certified Security Administrator (CCSA).

Personal competencies:

  • Strong collaboration and communication skills.
  • Initiative-driven with a solution-oriented mindset.
  • Detail-oriented and thorough in execution.
  • Curious and eager to grow professionally.
  • Fluent in English; a Nordic language is an advantage.

We are more than a workplace

Join Telia and our inclusive team and collaborate with over 15,000 talented colleagues from around the globe. Recognized as a Climate Leader in the Nordics and Baltics, we're passionate about creating great products and delivering extraordinary customer experiences while always prioritizing sustainability. We believe our people are our most valuable asset, ensuring everyone feels welcome and valued. When you join us, you'll receive a comprehensive benefits package tailored to your local market, opportunities for remote work, and more. You'll also have the chance to earn a bonus based on your performance and Telia's results. If you want to make a real impact, this is the place where you belong.

Ready to make better happen?

If you fancy joining our team, don’t hesitate to apply! If you want to know more about the job, reach out to Masoud Tondkar, masoud.tondkar@teliacompany.com

We look forward to receiving your application and to make sure that your personal data is kept safely, we kindly ask you to apply through our recruitment system. We are not able to handle resumes via e-mail or equivalent. Selection will take place on an ongoing basis and this position might be filled before the application deadline.

Application deadline is 21.09.2025

Final candidates will be subject to a security clearance and background check.

Join us and let's make better happen - together!

companyDescription:
At Relax Gaming, we're not just simplifying B2B iGaming content delivery – we're revolutionising it! Since 2010, our teams across the globe have earned a reputation for pioneering cutting-edge casino games while winning countless industry awards along the way.
 
Home to the legendary Money Train series and our game-changing Dream Drop Jackpots, which has created over 20 millionaires since 2022, our mission is to deliver unparalleled player experiences.
 
As proud drivers of differentiation within the iGaming industry, our reach extends further through our innovative aggregation platforms: Silver Bullet, which empowers hand-picked up-and-coming studios, and Powered By Relax, for seamless content distribution.
 
Connecting over 7,000 games to 900+ operator brands via one seamless integration, we're empowering the future of online entertainment globally. Join us in challenging the status quo!
descriptionHtml:
Join Relax Gaming’s growing Information Security team and play a key role in protecting one of the fastest-growing companies in iGaming.


At Relax Gaming, security isn’t just about compliance, it’s about enabling innovation while safeguarding our people, products, and players. We’re looking for a skilled Security Engineer who thrives in dynamic environments, enjoys solving complex problems, and wants to make a real impact across the business.


What you’ll do

As a Security Engineer at Relax, you’ll be part of a collaborative team that ensures our security objectives are met and that we remain ahead of emerging threats. Your work will span across proactive risk management, hands-on security operations, and supporting audits in regulated environments.


  • Protect & innovate: Design, implement, and manage security frameworks that safeguard Relax ICT systems.
  • Be hands-on: Conduct risk assessments, manage vulnerabilities, monitor logs, investigate incidents, and continuously improve our security posture.
  • Drive compliance: Support ISO 27001:2022, financial audits, and other compliance requirements while ensuring smooth remediation.
  • Enable the business: Create security policies, raise awareness, and act as a trusted advisor to teams across the organization.
  • Stay ahead: Keep up with evolving threats and bring best practices to strengthen our defenses.


What we’re looking for

We’re not just after certifications and checklists, we want someone passionate about security, with the curiosity to keep learning and the communication skills to bring others on the journey.


  • Proven expertise in Security Operations (log monitoring, threat detection, incident response).
  • Hands-on knowledge of EDR, SIEM, IDS/IPS, MDM, DDoS protection, and vulnerability management.
  • Audit experience, ideally ISO 27001, from preparation to remediation.
  • Relevant certifications (CISSP or equivalent required; ISO 27001 Lead Implementer preferred).
  • Technical edge: scripting/automation skills (Python, Bash, Jira/Confluence automation) are a plus.
  • Strong communicator & educator: able to explain complex security issues in plain English and influence stakeholders who may not have a security background.
  • Degree in Information Security, Computer Science, or a related field.


Why join Relax Gaming?

At Relax, you’ll join a diverse, international team united by curiosity, creativity, and drive. Security here isn’t an afterthought, it’s central to how we grow, operate, and innovate in regulated markets around the world.


  • Impact: Your work will directly shape how Relax Gaming operates securely across multiple jurisdictions.
  • Growth: With rapid expansion, there’s plenty of room for you to develop your skills and take on new responsibilities.
  • Collaboration: Work closely with teams across product, compliance, and operations in a supportive, respectful culture.
  • Flexibility: This role can be based in Malta, Estonia, Serbia, Sweden, or the Isle of Man.


Life at Relax in Malmö

Located in the heart of the city, just a few steps from Lilla Torg, our (two floor + terrace) Malmö office is more than just a workplace. Here, you’ll find a diverse, vibrant, family-like community. With a vibe in the office, that is always upbeat. Our modern space blends Scandinavian comfort with a fast-paced, international mindset. With excellent transport connections and all the best the city has to offer right outside the door, it's the perfect base for doing great work and enjoying life while you do it. Come for the innovation, stay for the people.


About Relax Gaming

Relax Gaming Group was founded in 2010 with the goal of creating games for the modern iGaming landscape. Always staying true to the Relax core values - Driven, Adaptable, Supportive, and Respectful - the recent and rapid expansion has been conceived in order to deliver unparalleled global reach. Via a quick one-time integration, Relax Gaming now provides access to a roster of 1500+ casino games and a diverse range of proprietary products, including Poker, Bingo, and its own rapidly expanding slot portfolio.


The high-quality aggregated content is provided through its selected Silver Bullet (commercially represented) and Powered By Relax (commercially independent) partners. Regulated markets are also at the heart of its growth strategy, with both fully supported regulated markets as well as licenses held in multiple jurisdictions.


This role can be located in almost every one of our locations: Malta, Estonia, Serbia, Sweden or the Isle of Man.

Keen to find out more? Learn about us here.

companyDescription:
Azets is an international business advisory group, with 9,000 local experts in 190 locations across eight countries, backed by progressive technology. We are united by one clear purpose: to improve the lives of our clients, colleagues and communities in a sustainable way.

100,000+ clients on unique journeys trust us to meet their immediate and evolving needs, remove barriers, and deliver sustained outcomes so they can move forward with confidence.

Your goals, our people, a fresh mindset – solving problems so you can confidently focus on what you do best.
companySlogan: Move forward with confidence.
descriptionHtml:
Om Azets

Azets är en internationell koncern som erbjuder tjänster och rådgivning inom lön, ekonomi och HR. I Sverige har bolaget cirka 800 medarbetare och en omsättning på omkring 1 miljard SEK och med totalt 9000 medarbetare inom koncernen.


Azets befinner sig i en förändringsresa och söker nu en Cyber Security Specialist som vill vara med och stärka samt vidareutveckla arbetet med cybersäkerhet och compliance. Hos Azets blir du en del av en organisation där du får möjlighet att påverka och driva förändring i nära samarbete med internationella kollegor och lokala team.


Om rollen

Detta är en kommersiellt inriktad roll där du använder din kunskap om säkerhetskoncept för att utveckla praktiska arbetssätt och processer som stärker verksamheten, bidrar till en säker produktdesign och skapar förtroende hos kunderna.

Rollen kommer ha ett nära samarbete med Risk & Compliance-teamet i frågor som rör cybersäkerhet, riskhantering och kunddialoger.


Du kommer bland annat:

  • Samverka med gruppfunktionen kring strategi och säkerhetsfrågor och förbättringsförslag för att utveckla lokala processer som stärker verksamheten i arbetssätt och rutiner.
  • Proaktivt jobba med utbildning och kunskapsdelning för att höja säkerhetsmedvetenheten och främja en säkerhetskultur internt.
  • Arbeta med riskbedömningar, assessments och audits för att identifiera förbättringsmöjligheter, effektivisera processer och stötta organisationen i säkra arbetssätt.
  • Föra dialog med leverantörer för att säkerställa att nya lösningar uppfyller säkerhetskrav och stödjer produktdesign.
  • Bidra till att utveckla incident response-planer samt delta i arbetet med att analysera cyberhot och följa utvecklingen inom säkerhetsområdet.


Om dig

Vi söker dig som har god förståelse för cybersäkerhet och compliance och som tycker om att arbeta nära kunder, stakeholders och leverantörer. Du är kommunikativ, trygg i dialoger och bygger förtroende med olika målgrupper.


Vi tror att du har:

  • 1-3 års erfarenhet inom cyber-säkerhet, compliance eller närliggande område.
  • Förståelse för olika säkerhetskoncept samt erfarenhet av att arbeta med policies, dokumentation och efterlevnad.
  • Kunskap om standarder och regelverk, exempelvis ISO27001, NIS2, NIST, GDPR och säkerhetsskyddslagen.
  • Mycket goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska.
  • Erfarenhet från internationella bolag.


Azets samarbetar med Safemind för denna rekrytering.

Med mer än 3 miljoner användare förenklar Dstny kommunikationen för företag, partners och tjänsteleverantörer med interaktiva verktyg som levereras som en tjänst i alla format, inklusive röst, video och chatt. Dstnys innovativa teknologi och starka lokala partnerskap har en mobil-först-design och enkel integrering som gör det möjligt att leverera exceptionella användarupplevelser. Dstny har sitt huvudkontor i Bryssel och har ca. 1000 anställda i 7 europeiska länder och en årlig omsättning på ca. 250 miljoner euro. Läs mer på www.dstny.se.
descriptionHtml:
Join Dstny – Europe's Leading Cloud Communications Provider

Dstny is the largest European-based cloud communications software provider, delivering a Unified Communications-as-a-Service (UCaaS) platform to customers.

As our installation base continues to grow, we are looking for an experienced R&D DevOps Engineer to join our team and help elevate our customer-focused services to new heights. The emphasis for this role is on the R&D part of DevOps, meaning focusing on deciding the deployment strategies for systems that are managed by the Operations team.

About The Role

As a DevOps Engineer, you will play a key role both in developing new functionality and automating deployments during the development phases in the development environments. You will collaborate with R&D and Operations teams to enhance deployment automation, improve monitoring, and drive efficiency across our infrastructure.

What You’ll Do

  • On a daily basis, the DevOps Engineer works with the developers in R&D, helping create and deploy applications in Kubernetes
  • Develop micro-services that are required for other applications and services at the framework level
  • Automate deployment processes and manage CI/CD pipelines using tools like GitLab CI, Terraform, Ansible, Jenkins, etc.
  • Work closely with R&D and Ops teams to enhance automation and improve existing infrastructure.
  • Continuously refine deployment strategies to increase efficiency and minimize errors.
  • Identify production issues and develop automated monitoring solutions based on Grafana monitoring stack.
  • Help Operations teams to monitor and troubleshoot production environments to ensure high availability and performance.
  • Participate in incident response and post-incident analysis to enhance system reliability.
  • Collaborate with software developers to optimize infrastructure for new features and applications.

Who You Are

We are looking for a team player with great collaboration and communication skills. You can find best in class solutions through collaboration with different teams. You are self-driven and engaged with a will to encourage people as well as resilient and result-driven with a persuasive, flexible, and passionate personality.

What You Need

  • 3+ years of experience as a DevOps Engineer or similar role.
  • Hands-on experience with AWS, Kubernetes deployments, using helm / helmchart, Terraform, Terragrunt, Ansible, and GitOps based pipelines on GitLab CI.
  • Strong knowledge of monitoring stack based on Grafana stack (Loki, Prometheus and Tempo), and Alertmanager / OpsGenie, experience in working with monitoring systems, building integrations, dashboards and alerts.
  • Prior experience in developing and running Java Springboot based microservices.
  • Prior experience in running or supporting business critical workloads running on Kubernetes in production environments, addressing the needs for performance and latency, and capacity planning.
  • Proficiency in Linux-based infrastructure and scripting for automation.
  • Experience in database management (e.g., MySQL, MongoDB).
  • Excellent troubleshooting skills and a problem-solving mindset.
  • Familiarity with open-source tools and automation technologies.
  • Bonus: Experience in telecommunications (SIP, PBX), virtualization platforms (VMware) and Keycloak and any asynchronous messaging systems, building intrusion detection or prevention systems, building pipelines using Argo CD.

Why Join Dstny?

  • Work with a talented and passionate team of DevOps, Ops, and R&D professionals.
  • Gain exposure to cutting-edge technologies in cloud communications.
  • Enjoy a flexible work environment with opportunities for growth.
  • Be part of an internationally diverse company with a strong culture.
  • Competitive salary and benefits package.

About Dstny

At Dstny, we believe in connecting people, fostering collaboration, and enabling seamless communication through innovative technology.

As Europe’s leading cloud communications provider, we serve Service Providers across Europe, Asia, and Africa. With over 60% market share in the Nordics, we’ve reshaped the communications industry, partnering with top-tier providers like Telia, Tele2, and Hi3G.

Our feature-rich UCaaS platform is designed for SMEs, Enterprises, and mobile markets, offering seamless fixed-mobile convergence and multi-tenancy capabilities. We do this by an advanced Service Mediation Gateway, enabling rapid development of new applications.

We are constantly innovating, evolving, and looking for talented professionals to help drive our next phase of growth.

With our iCORE principles, we distinguish ourselves from other employers:

  • Innovation: I innovate to stay ahead
  • Commitment: I'm committed from start to finish
  • Obsession for CX: I'm obsessed with customer experience
  • Respect: I have respect for everyone’s rhythm, values, and talents
  • Empower: I empower the people around me

Come join us and be part of the future of cloud communications!

We at Dstny are a European leader and innovator in secure cloud communications, based on our powerful UCaaS offer and technology. Over 2 million business users are empowered to communicate, collaborate, and provide excellent customer service with our tools at hand. We enable service providers, partners, and end-users as well as 3rd party services to thrive in our ecosystem. With headquarters in Brussels, we have more than 700 employees in 7 European countries.

companyDescription:
Our mission is to empower exceptional lawyers. 

We’re building the world’s first truly collaborative AI for legal professionals: a workspace for boundless collaboration between lawyer ingenuity and machine intelligence. 

We're serving 250+ clients across the globe and are backed by ICONIQ, General Catalyst, Benchmark, Redpoint, Y Combinator, and other top investors.
companySlogan: Collaborative AI for lawyers
descriptionHtml:
About Us

At Legora, we’re on a mission to transform the way lawyers work. Our AI-native platform lets legal professionals enhance their productivity and automate complex workflows. We collaborate closely with our clients and iterate at a market-leading pace. In a year, we have gone from an early MVP to tools used daily by thousands of lawyers, and are now scaling rapidly. We are here to win together with our team and clients, and carry the belief that life is too short not to accomplish something truly special.

You are joining an early stage scale-up with exceptional product-market fit, trusted by global leaders like Bird & Bird, Pérez-Llorca, and Mannheimer Swartling. Backed by over $80M in funding from top investors including Benchmark, Redpoint, and Y Combinator. And a a world-class team from Google, Klarna, Spotify, and top tier law firms with infinite room to grow professionally.

The role

As a Platform Engineer at Legora, you’ll join an enabling team focused on making life easier for developers. Your mission: improve reliability, boost performance, and build a rock-solid platform that scales with the company. You'll work hands-on across infrastructure and backend systems, helping teams ship faster, safer, and smarter.

What You Will Be Doing

  • Build the core infrastructure and reliability abstractions that all our engineering teams use
  • Sit with all teams and help them structure their SRE efforts: observability, alerting, fault tolerance and reliability
  • Proactively monitor our infrastructure and develop it for hyperscale and reliability
  • Contribute to incident response and post-incident learning
  • Work with Pulumi on primarily Azure, but also AI deployments on AWS and GCP

Who you are

  • You have years of experience as a backend engineer but have solid infrastructure, observability, monitoring know-how
  • Experience with Pulumi (preferred) or similar IaC tools like AWS CDK, Terraform
  • But most importantly, you are someone who
    • “Gets stuff done” and understands building a $10bn company isn’t always glamorous and takes hard work and long hours
    • Proactively and independently solves the most burning problems
    • strives in a fast-paced environment where the answers aren’t always clear and processes are few
What's in it for you

  • Career development opportunities in a rapidly evolving entrepreneurial environment.
  • Direct collaboration with senior leadership and the opportunity to directly impact company growth and strategy.
  • A collaborative, high-energy team environment where your ideas and contributions are valued and implemented.
  • Competitive salary complemented with a transparent and highly competitive options program.
  • Centrally located offices in Stockholm, designed as a space for you to do your life's work.

We founded Legora to empower lawyers with the tools they truly deserve. We’re not building for the way law is practiced today - we’re reimagining how it will be practiced tomorrow.

You'll work hands-on across infrastructure and backend systems, helping teams ship faster, safer, and smarter.What You Will Be DoingBuild the core infrastructure and reliability abstractions that all our engineering teams useSit with all teams and help them structure their SRE efforts: observability, alerting, fault tolerance and reliabilityProactively monitor our infrastructure and develop it for hyperscale and reliabilityContribute to incident response and post-incident learningWork with Pulumi on primarily Azure, but also AI deployments on AWS and GCPWho you areYou have years of experience as a backend engineer but have solid infrastructure, observability, monitoring know-howExperience with Pulumi (preferred) or similar IaC tools like AWS CDK, TerraformBut most importantly, you are someone who“Gets stuff done” and understands building a $10bn company isn’t always glamorous and takes hard work and long hoursProactively and independently solves the most burning problemsstrives in a fast-paced environment where the answers aren’t always clear and processes are fewWhat's in it for youCareer development opportunities in a rapidly evolving entrepreneurial environment.Direct collaboration with senior leadership and the opportunity to directly impact company growth and strategy.A collaborative, high-energy team environment where your ideas and contributions are valued and implemented.Competitive salary complemented with a transparent and highly competitive options program.Centrally located offices in Stockholm, designed as a space for you to do your life's work.We founded Legora to empower lawyers with the tools they truly deserve. We’re not building for the way law is practiced today - we’re reimagining how it will be practiced tomorrow.
